fact: September 9 is exactly 252 days into the year - that's 36 weeks of pure calendar magic!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels typically go into overdrive around this time, frantically burying nuts like tiny furry pirates hiding treasure for winter.
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around September 9, making it super easy to find directions without a compass.
In Canada, maple trees often start their preparation for fall colors around this date, as cooler nights trigger changes in their sap.
September 9 falls in the middle of Virgo season, when the constellation Virgo dominates the night sky.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of spring - flowers are blooming while the north thinks about fall!
Monarch butterflies are typically in full migration mode around September 9 in North America, traveling up to 100 miles per day on their epic journey south.
The autumnal equinox is only about two weeks away from this date, meaning day and night are getting closer to being perfectly equal.
September 9 has the same number of letters as the month it's in - both have exactly 9 letters!
In northern regions, spider webs often reach peak beauty around September 9, creating those dewdrop-covered masterpieces you see sparkling in morning grass.
September 9 is usually getting closer to the harvest moon - the brightest and most famous full moon of the year.
